Report Overview

Binary Classification SHAP Analysis Full QA Report
This AutoQuant-native report renders precomputed Binary Classification SHAP artifacts. It summarizes existing Shap_ contribution columns toward the configured positive class; it does not compute SHAP values, call predict(), require a model object, or use a SHAP backend package.

Methodology
SHAP values are expected to be produced upstream by AutoQuant modeling or scoring workflows. This renderer consumes the structured artifact output from generate_binary_classification_shap_analysis_artifacts() and does not rebuild analysis inside the Rmd.
Artifact inventory
Plot counts represent AutoPlots/htmlwidget artifacts. Tables are rendered with the same reactable/table-wrap pattern used by existing AutoQuant artifact reports.

SHAP Overview

Overview
Binary Classification SHAP Analysis
Model: not supplied
Data: not supplied
Rows: 300
Valid SHAP columns: 3
Mapped source features: 3
Target column: Target
Prediction column: Predict
Predicted class column: PredictedClass
Positive class: Yes
Prediction scale: probability
Threshold: 0.5
DateVar/date aggregation: Date / month
ByVars: Factor_1
Top N: 3
SHAP values were precomputed upstream. This generator only summarizes existing SHAP contribution columns.
SHAP values are interpreted as contributions toward or away from the positive class under the upstream modeling contract.
Signed SHAP values are reported on the supplied prediction scale: probability
Diagnostics / Config
SHAP Column Map

Global Importance

Global importance ranks features by mean absolute SHAP contribution. Categorical levels and binned numeric levels are shown separately so level-specific effects are not hidden inside one feature-level total.
Global importance visualizations
Binary Global SHAP Importance Plot
bar / global_importance
Binary SHAP Distribution Plot
box / shap_distribution
Categorical / Binned Numeric Level SHAP Importance Plot
bar / categorical_level_importance
Categorical / Binned Numeric Level SHAP Distribution Plot
box / categorical_level_importance
open Global importance table
open Categorical / binned numeric level importance table

Threshold Context

Threshold context artifacts summarize prediction-score behavior around the configured classification threshold.
Threshold visualizations
Binary Threshold SHAP Context Plot
bar / threshold_context
open Threshold context table

Class Balance / Outcome Context

Class balance and outcome context artifacts make the positive class, observed outcomes, predictions, and feature-level class differences explicit.
Class balance visualizations
Class-Specific SHAP Importance Plot
bar / class_balance
open Class balance table
open Class feature importance table

Single Feature Effects

Single feature effects summarize SHAP contributions by source feature values or bins. Categorical bar charts use the same horizontal convention as importance charts.

SHAP Dependence

Dependence artifacts show feature values, SHAP contributions, and optional prediction, target, ID, date, or segment context.

Segment Effects

ByVars are segment lenses. Segment heatmaps show signed mean SHAP by feature and segment, so red/blue direction is retained instead of showing only magnitude.
Segment visualizations
Binary Segment Mean SHAP Heatmap: Factor_1
heatmap / segment_effects
Segment effects table

Time Effects

Time effects use DateVar aggregation when available. Aggregation: month.
Time visualizations
Binary Time SHAP Effects Plot
line / time_effects
Binary Time SHAP Effects Heatmap
heatmap / time_effects
Time effects table

Local Explanations

Local explanations summarize top contributors toward and away from the configured positive class for selected row indexes. Contribution bars use the same horizontal bar convention as global importance.
Local explanation visualizations
Local Binary SHAP Contributions: Row 1
bar / local_explanations
Local Binary SHAP Contributions: Row 2
bar / local_explanations
Local explanations table

Interaction Importance

Methodology
Binary SHAP interaction diagnostics
These diagnostics use precomputed ordinary Shap_ columns and existing source feature columns.
Numeric variables are binned and categorical/date variables use bounded levels.
Two-way surfaces place the SHAP-attributed feature's actual value/bin on one axis and the interaction lens feature's actual value/bin on the other axis.
Heatmap values are signed mean SHAP for the attributed feature across those actual value/bin combinations.
Scores are candidate interaction diagnostics based on binned level-combination heterogeneity, not exact SHAP interaction values.
Exact pairwise SHAP interaction values require upstream interaction-specific output.
Interaction visualizations
Binary Candidate Interaction Ranking Plot

open Candidate interaction ranking
Two-way SHAP surface table
These are binned/leveled candidate interaction diagnostics from ordinary Shap_ columns. Surface axes are actual source feature value bins/levels, labeled with the pair variables. Heatmap values are signed mean SHAP for the attributed feature. They are not exact SHAP interaction value decompositions.
